dimensions.rationale: Frozen S2 specification revision 7 is READY and every required requirement is projected into required workstreams with exact planned-item statements after projection repair.

The plan separates core lifecycle semantics from adapters, adds the modular controller boundary, keeps adapters as host-operation projections, and reserves release/finalization authority to controller-owned workstreams.
Sixteen workstreams have disjoint write sets; shared release/finalization/controller artifacts are owned by explicit sequential workstreams; writeOverlapCount is zero.
Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Hermes and OpenCode are covered by a shared offline EXPERIMENTAL adapter baseline, with live VERIFIED promotion explicitly outside required release proof.
Neutrality v3 freezes external no-follow signed authority, write-once output projection, redaction, stale/input rejection, release neutrality and finalization neutrality checks.
The resource budget profile remains frozen; max task context is 38899 bytes under the 49152-byte ceiling, validation invocations are explicit, and estimated minimum tool calls are bounded at 81.
WS-01 evidence includes controller primary artifacts plus protocol outputs for local neutrality scan, negative suite and foundation suite; local test proof passed before review.
The release matrix, aggregate receipts, immutable release assembly, README/license/governance scope and installability boundaries are assigned to WS-14 with final audit in WS-15.

Revision 9 removes the WS-01 approval-bearing host-operation and replaces pytest with local stdlib commands that consume signed controller operation requests and write declared protocol outputs. Approval-bearing downstream/release work remains gated separately.
WS-01 startup is portable to clean Python 3.11-3.13 environments without optional pytest, and context remains under budget after r9 contextRef reduction.

findings.message: Revision 7 replaces the ambiguous self-scanning neutrality boundary with operation-bound write-once output projection, external signed authority, no-replace publication, stable reads and negative evidence.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve neutrality v3 semantics and fail closed on occupancy, alias, race, mutation, stale authority and signature mismatch.

findings.message: Revision 7 assigns WS-14 release assembly to produce a non-recursive candidatePayloadInventoryDigest before release neutrality and a separate finalInventoryDigest for the committed generation.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve WS-14 sequential ownership, release assembly transaction order and crash/substitution/matrix mismatch negative evidence.

findings.message: Revision 7 moves terminal completion through READY_FOR_FINALIZATION and AWAITING_FINAL_PROOF with final proof body/envelope separation, finalization output projection and WAL/state binding before COMPLETE.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve final proof parity, post-review mutation negatives and COMPLETE CAS/recovery semantics.

findings.message: WS-01 keeps the neutrality subpackage entrypoint while WS-07 owns root CLI/module entrypoints.
Specification and plan-review ancestry is append-only and content-addressed through r07/r05.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve review and freeze lineage during plan freeze.

findings.message: Bootstrap, runtime and release neutrality scopes, external trust inputs, signed receipts, zero-result counters and redaction are present.
Task and final acceptance reject unresolved Medium, High or Blocker findings.
Required adapters run offline from frozen contracts and cannot claim maturity above EXPERIMENTAL without separate live proof; Hermes is now included.
findings.requiredAction: Keep live capability outside the required DAG and preserve evidence-accurate maturity.

findings.message: Product profile freezes S0/S1/S2 ceilings, benchmark cohorts, regression limits and bounded live calibration.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve product budget and benchmark authorities.

findings.message: Nine CI-owned platform legs, external authority and fail-closed aggregate evidence are frozen.
findings.requiredAction: Preserve all leg identities, receipts and aggregate consumption.

findings.message: Workstreams remain the sole canonical dependency DAG; worker-level dependsOn fields remain absent.

Revision 7 execution-controller init rejected approval-bearing local-process validation commands. Revision 8 converts every host-policy or required validation command to provider-neutral host-operation dispatch with independent receipt and approval authorities; approval=none deterministic checks remain local-process.
findings.requiredAction: Keep approval-bearing controller validation commands as host-operation; do not reintroduce local-process execution for commands requiring host-policy or required approval.

findings.message: Revision 9 resolves the WS-01 bootstrap autonomy gap by converting neutrality bootstrap evidence from host-operation/pytest to local Python stdlib controller-validation commands with protocol outputs, while keeping downstream/release origin-specific signed neutrality gates mandatory.
findings.requiredAction: Freeze revision 9, recompile task packets under the new plan digest, and start a new or controlled-remediated run attempt instead of reusing the blocked r8 validation operation.

summary: READY_TO_FREEZE: revision 9 is production-plan-ready for the next execution attempt. WS-01 no longer depends on pytest or host-operation approval; downstream/release origin-specific signed neutrality remains mandatory.
optimizationNotes: WS-01 contextRefs were reduced to four files to keep maxTaskContextBytes below the 49152-byte budget.
WS-01 validation commands use Python stdlib runner/scanner and controller protocol outputs for inspectable evidence.
The plan preserves schema 3 controller-validation and does not rewrite downstream DAG ownership.
